title: cicuta maculata var. californica
intro: Cicuta maculata var. californica, also known as California water hemlock, is a highly toxic and poisonous plant native to the western coast of North America. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to induce emesis, a natural purging of the body. The plant's extracts are also believed to have health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and acting as a diuretic, although these claims have not been extensively scientifically proven. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often dried and used to make teas or tinctures, although extreme caution is advised due to its highly toxic nature.
